eval_dimensions.name: Override Discovery
eval_dimensions.description: Finds all user override files and matches them to their default playbooks by filename

eval_dimensions.name: Drift Detection
eval_dimensions.description: Compares override steps against defaults by subject matching, detects new steps, prompt changes, skills changes, fragment drift, and intentional divergences

eval_dimensions.name: Fragment Comparison
eval_dimensions.description: Expands fragments before comparing, handles differently-named fragments as intentional divergence, and detects new fragment steps

eval_dimensions.name: Decision Gates
eval_dimensions.description: All blocking choices use AskUserQuestion tool calls, not plain text questions

eval_dimensions.name: Task Orchestration
eval_dimensions.description: Creates phase tasks at startup and updates status as each phase completes

evals.name: No overrides found
evals.description: User invokes skill in a project with no playbook overrides, expects clean exit message

assertions.signal: Reports no overrides found when none exist

evals.name: Detect new default step missing from override
evals.description: Default playbook has a step that the user override does not, skill reports it as INFO severity

assertions.signal: Identifies steps in defaults not present in override

evals.name: Detect fragment step count difference
evals.description: Default fragment has more steps than user's shadowing fragment

assertions.signal: Reports fragment drift when step counts differ

evals.name: Intentional divergence classified as INFO
evals.description: Override uses a differently-named fragment (e.g., shipping-pipeline-solo vs shipping-pipeline), classified as INFO not MEDIUM

assertions.signal: Intentional divergences are INFO severity, not MEDIUM

evals.name: Phase 3 uses AskUserQuestion for action choice
evals.description: After presenting findings, skill must use AskUserQuestion with structured options

assertions.signal: AskUserQuestion is called at Phase 3 action choice point

evals.name: Phase 4 review-and-apply uses AskUserQuestion per finding
evals.description: When user chooses 'Review and apply', each finding gets an AskUserQuestion gate

assertions.signal: AskUserQuestion is called for each finding in review mode

evals.name: Creates phase tasks at startup
evals.description: Skill creates 4 phase tasks before any work begins

assertions.signal: TaskCreate is called for each of the 4 phases

evals.name: Filter to specific skill when argument provided
evals.description: User passes a skill name as argument, only that skill's overrides are analyzed

assertions.signal: Only the specified skill is analyzed when argument is provided
